named_npcs.name: Avery Choi
named_npcs.description: A brilliant scientist who works for EONS, and often provides information about the organization's inside dealings to Candela Obscura.

named_npcs.name: Caerwyn Stone
named_npcs.description: A young, newly enlisted soldier in the Halen Armed Forces.

named_npcs.name: Chaska Deloria
named_npcs.description: Runs a well-known stand at the Calinus Market in Groundswell. They sell a wide variety of goods and can track down almost anything for the right price.

named_npcs.name: Dr. Michele Lappin
named_npcs.description: A serious doctor with a booming voice who works at the Grayslate Sanatorium in the Sidle.

named_npcs.name: Elder Kenneth Krause
named_npcs.description: An elder in the Ascendancy with a suspected connection to the Red Hand.

named_npcs.name: Ember Rose
named_npcs.description: A burlesque performer at the Library in Red Lamp. She is known for her impressive acrobatics performance during the 'Banned & Censored' show.

named_npcs.name: Father Timothy Singh
named_npcs.description: A priest with possible ties to EONS who works out of a small church still standing on the edge of Westwreck.

named_npcs.name: Joseph Abdi
named_npcs.description: A surprisingly chipper metro worker at the Seren Street Station in the Varnish.

named_npcs.name: Kiana Tumata
named_npcs.description: A run-down dock worker employed in Hallowharbor.

named_npcs.name: Oksana Blum
named_npcs.description: A well-known medium who claims to connect with spirits and relay messages from beyond the grave.

named_npcs.name: Richard Satanta
named_npcs.description: A disgruntled employee at the famous Eternal Heart Spa in the Eaves.

named_npcs.name: Alma Baquiran
named_npcs.description: A guard at the Brinkley Penitentiary who secretly delivers messages into and out of the prison for the inmates.

named_npcs.name: Magistra Elara Voss
named_npcs.description: Her archive contains the only surviving records of the Pale Door's earlier manifestations in this city. She has read them. She was affected.

named_npcs.name: Cpl. Benedikt Haase
named_npcs.description: He patrols the area where the Moth Swarm first appeared. He has seen things he cannot explain. He keeps detailed unofficial notes.

named_npcs.name: Dr. Mireille Chantry
named_npcs.description: She has treated victims of the Crimson Weave and keeps tissue samples in a sealed cold cabinet. She will share them if asked directly.

named_npcs.name: Sable
named_npcs.description: She moves alchemical materials for the city's underground and knows who is buying the components for the Crimson Weave. She will not say unless the circle earns her trust.

named_npcs.name: Professor Aldric Mourn
named_npcs.role: Chair of Natural Philosophy, Ashford University
named_npcs.description: He is the city's foremost theoretical expert on dimensional phenomena and will share his knowledge freely — but his firsthand account is the real prize.

named_npcs.name: Sister Ines Collard
named_npcs.role: Church Warden, Basilica of the First Flame
named_npcs.description: The Bone Singer manifested inside the Basilica twice. Both times she interpreted it as a divine visitation. Her records are extraordinarily detailed.

named_npcs.name: Gregor Lenz
named_npcs.description: He knows the exact counter-reagent for the Crimson Weave and the Glass Garden. He will provide it if the circle explains the situation without telling him it was his formula.

named_npcs.name: Theodora Vane
named_npcs.description: Her salon is a gathering point for occult enthusiasts. Three of the Thought Plague's primary early vectors attended her parties. She has not yet been infected, but she is close.

named_npcs.name: Nils Acker
named_npcs.description: His archives contain the most comprehensive spatial data on phenomena locations in the city's history. He will share it enthusiastically — he has been desperate for someone to explain the patterns.

named_npcs.name: The Caretaker
named_npcs.role: Unknown — appears near Pale Door scar locations
named_npcs.description: The Caretaker knows more about the Pale Door than any living person. They will share nothing until they have observed the circle across at least two investigations and judged them worthy.

named_npcs.name: Constance Frey
named_npcs.description: Her published articles have spread awareness of the Thought Plague city-wide. She is simultaneously a valuable information source and an active vector. The circle must decide how to handle this.

named_npcs.name: Warden Kaske
named_npcs.description: His morgue contains victims of at least four distinct phenomena. He keeps meticulous intake records and will allow examination of remains if the circle presents any official-looking credentials.

light_eater.name: Tang Yuna
light_eater.description: The foreman for the logging company, Hackley & Wilde, is desperate to get the situation under control.

light_eater.name: Nigel Munro
light_eater.description: The owner of the only bar in town, the Bridle Bar.

light_eater.name: Alice Shelley
light_eater.description: A widow who prefers traditional candlelight instead of modern electricity.

light_eater.name: Lucia Rodriguez
light_eater.description: A logger who's worked for the Hackley & Wilde Timber Co. for nearly a decade. She has seen it all and then some.

still_life.name: Oscar Enfield
still_life.description: A recently widowed death photographer who has unknowingly been using the Eye of Emrys.

still_life.name: Sara Khalil
still_life.description: The owner of The Cracked Lens, a Groundswell camera repair and supply shop that last handled the artifact.

still_life.name: Jacob Finsky
still_life.description: The owner of a bodega in Groundswell near Mx. Khalil's camera shop, whose cat was recently photographed by Oscar.

still_life.name: Chen Kai
still_life.description: A priest tasked with tending to those who visit the Sacred Ascension Cemetery, who's been receiving alarming reports from groundskeepers.

devils_well.name: Emerson Walsh
devils_well.description: The radio operator who received the distress signals from the dirigible before its crash.

devils_well.name: Bradley Devir
devils_well.description: The daring ship captain taking the team out into the mist.

devils_well.name: Emilia Kowalska
devils_well.description: An EONS scientist whose notes and audio recordings can be found throughout the Devil's Well.

devils_well.name: Tarek Abbas
devils_well.description: A former member of EONS charged with caring for the Reliquary. He hid a journal within the walls of the facility.

stage_fright.name: Luis Roe
stage_fright.description: OUP officer who calls in Candela Obscura. Luis has no qualms about doing what needs to be done to solve the case.

stage_fright.name: Rosie Sinclair
stage_fright.description: Coworker of the victim, Andres Aguilar. She and Andres are long-time friends and she is worried about his well-being.

stage_fright.name: Rokas Novak
stage_fright.description: Food cart operator who worked outside Andres's office. They know everyone who works in the area and have a keen eye for detail.

stage_fright.name: Usman Saba
stage_fright.description: Former crew member of the Eden Theater who still works in the area. His memory isn't what it used to be.
